text:
Market today: Mild risk-on tone, tech led the first half and chips are trying to hold. But headline #7 is the one that matters for you - a threat to the chip rally just hit a 9-year high. That's a direct headwind for your heaviest sector exposure. Fed's Warsh also killed any rate-cut hope from the next meeting, which keeps pressure on growth/multiple names.

Your holdings: SOXL (-6.48%) is your loudest problem - it's a 3X leveraged chip ETF, and with margin on top, a continued chip-sector slide hits you twice as hard; stop is $200, watch it closely. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard - stop at $9.50, know your exit. SMH (-0.20%) is flat but tied to the same chip-threat headline.

Watch: That chip-rally threat headline - if it materializes into selling, SMH and SOXL move fast and ugly. Also watch whether CRWD holds above $760 after its scanner setup.

Bottom line: Protect SOXL and PTNM before hunting new trades - margin makes the downside non-negotiable.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it, no exceptions. On margin with a 3X leveraged ETF in a sector facing a 9-year high threat, a break below $200 needs an immediate exit, not a hope.
PTNM: Down 15.25% and near its $9.50 stop. Decide now what you're doing if it hits - tiny specs on margin don't get second chances. No new buys today - hold and watch.
